items.title: Secret police recordings used in Matt Wright's trial released to the media

children.content: Secretly recorded phone calls and conversations crucial to the criminal case against celebrity crocodile wrangler Matt Wright during his Northern Territory Supreme Court trial have been released to the media.

gallery.altText: Close-up shot of Matt Wright's side profile as he walks into Darwin Local Court wearing a light blue shirt.

thumbnail.altText: Close-up shot of Matt Wright's side profile as he walks into Darwin Local Court wearing a light blue shirt.

avatar.alt: A journalist standing inside a television news studio, she is wearing a white blazer and smiling at the camera.

items.title: NT shadow minister admits underage son drove car, considers resignation

children.content: Manuel Brown has admitted his underage son drove his personal car, one week after failing to disclose the matter when asked by the media, and says he is considering resigning from his shadow portfolios.

image.altText: an aboriginal man wearing a suit in parliament

items.title: Former St Mary's Hostel site recognised as key Stolen Generations place

children.content: Former residents say the heritage listing of the St Mary's Hostel site in Alice Springs is an important step towards healing, truth-telling and remembering the children taken there under assimilation-era policies.

image.altText: A woman in a light grey jumper wearing sunglasses stands in front of a large tree

items.title: Why Alice Springs has been dubbed the lesbian capital of Australia

children.content: New research places Alice Springs in the top 10 most inclusive Australian cities for LGBTQIA+ people, cementing the outback town's long-held reputation as the lesbian capital of the country.

items.title: 'Deadly' Indigenous remote artists travel to Sydney

children.content: Remote Indigenous artists from around Australia are in Sydney for the annual National Indigenous Art Fair.

image.altText: Woman putting up an artwork in the exhibition.

items.title: Alice Springs drug and alcohol rehab curbed due to flooding

children.content: Drug and Alcohol Services Australia is running programs at a vastly reduced capacity due to flooding at its Alice Springs site earlier this year.

image.altText: The camera looks across the street at the DASA site behind a green fence, with cars out the front on the curb.

items.title: Cattle sale in Alice Springs nets $7m as market surges

children.content: The strong results at the Alice Springs sale reflect the overall Australian cattle market, which has surged in recent weeks.

image.altText: a close up image of a black steer

items.title: Today in history: NT town of Katherine gazetted 100 years ago

children.content: Today marks 100 years of the NT town of Katherine, which has a rich history as a meeting place and crossroads to the north and as home to some of Australia's most stunning natural sites.

image.altText: A group of Russian men and women are sitting on a tractor in a historical photo

items.title: Injuries and fires reported after NT cracker night celebrations

children.content: People across the Northern Territory have spent the night setting off their own fireworks for the Territory's annual cracker night celebrations, lighting up the sky with colour and keeping firefighters busy putting out blazes.

items.title: Man who spent eight days stuck under collapsed shopping centre in Venezuela rescued alive

children.content: Hernan Alberto Gil Flores, a 43-year-old Venezuelan man was dug out alive from under the ruins of a collapsed shopping centre, eight days after devastating earthquakes shook the nation.

image.altText: Rescue workers and group of people surround ambulance.

articles.title: 'What's really going on' inside Venezuela? The ABC hears the worst is to come

children.content: On the ground in Venezuela, death and desperation linger but they are juxtaposed against miracle survival stories.

image.altText: A man crying while sitting on a wall.

articles.title: Micro 'cockroach' drones help find earthquake survivors in Venezuela

children.content: A week after devastating twin earthquakes struck Venezuela rescue teams are using drones, dogs, cameras and specialised listening equipment to search for survivors.

items.title: Try-scoring whiz scores another three as Panthers snap losing run

items.title: Speculation of 'reconciliation' as Prince Harry prepares to travel to UK

items.title: Ohio town in shock after 16 kids found in squalor 'right under our noses'

items.title: Renewables company ZEN Energy enters voluntary administration

children.content: ZEN Energy was once an emerging player in the nation's renewables transition but has now entered voluntary administration, saying its viability had been impacted by "wholesale electricity volatility".

image.altText: Powerlines suspended between towers against orange dusk  sky.

items.title: 'Similar to a blender': Gina Rinehart defends helipad amid noise concerns

children.content: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart wants to be able to land a helicopter on the roof of her corporate HQ, and says the noise experienced by nearby residents would be similar to using a coffee machine or blender.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart smiles as she is interviewed by a journalist.

items.title: Can the Socceroos beat Egypt?

children.content: Can the Socceroos beat Egypt and win their first ever knockout final at a World Cup?
The Pharaohs have Salah, Marmoush and a dangerous counterattack, but the Socceroos have the physical edge, aerial threats and set-piece power to cause serious problems.
We take a closer look Egypt's strengths, and why Australia are a dangerous underdog that shouldn't be written off.

items.title: Meeting to decide Moira Deeming's future cancelled amid court battle
